tailoring_cover_letter_hook: In 2015, I sat in a Jakarta office watching a CFD broker's back-office system crash mid-settlement cycle. The PSP integration I had built kept retrying, the reconciliation failed silently, and 200 client withdrawals hung in limbo. I stayed up 36 hours straight, rebuilt the transaction pipeline with proper idempotency keys and dead-letter queues, and every withdrawal landed by market open. That night taught me something I have carried through 11 years of building fintech infrastructure for brokers like Pepperstone and TitanFX: capital market systems do not get second chances. Every transaction, every KYC check, every settlement must work the first time. Alta Exchange is building digital securities infrastructure for markets where trust is the scarcest resource, and I want to bring that hard-won reliability mindset to your Indonesia engineering team.

tailoring_cover_letter_hook: In 2013, I sat across from a Wirecard VP who told me, "If this payment gateway goes down at 3 AM, someone in Frankfurt loses a million euros before breakfast." That conversation rewired how I think about architecture. It stopped being about which framework or which cloud — and started being about resilience, traceability, and the discipline of designing systems that earn the trust of people who stake real money on them staying up. When I read that Microsoft is looking for a Cloud Solution Architect to help customers migrate, modernize, and secure their infrastructure on Azure, I recognized the same challenge at a different scale: organizations that need someone who has lived through migration failures, security incidents, and 3 AM escalations — not someone who has only read about them in a certification study guide.
